the device is modeled as a three-dimensional shape that can be described by the following parametric equations  
x(u, v) = a sin(u) cos(v)  
y(u, v) = a sin(u) sin(v)  
z(u, v) = b cos(u)  
where 0 ≤ u ≤ π and 0 ≤ v < 2π  
given that the surface area of this shape must be exactly 1000 square units to ensure optimal heat dissipation  
find the values of a and b that satisfy this condition  

the surface area of this shape is calculated using the surface integral of the function that describes the surface of the device  
the surface area is given by the integral over the domain of u and v of the magnitude of the cross product of the partial derivatives of the parametric equations
